title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, full-time contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York in 2015 and spent three years making weekend commutes from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin by himself. He added more cabins over time, turning the site into luxury Airbnb rentals listed at about $700/night and, as of September 2025, the property has generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: Purchased five-acre property in upstate New York in 2015.
Built the first A-frame cabin himself via weekend commutes from Connecticut over three years.
Expanded the site with additional cabins to create a multi-unit short-term rental offering.
Listings positioned as luxury cabins at roughly $700 per night.
Property earned over $2.1 million by September 2025.
takeaways: Given the conversion of a rural five-acre site into high-revenue short-term rentals, planners should note that similar developments can create concentrated seasonal demand for power and connectivity.
Because the project grew incrementally (single-owner construction then added units), infrastructure needs may emerge gradually rather than all at once—helpful for staged capacity planning.
High-end short-term rentals like $700/night cabins imply guests expect reliable utilities and internet, so dependable local grid and broadband capacity can be a differentiator.
Remote or rural hospitality developments that generate significant revenue may justify targeted investments in resilience and local distribution upgrades.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Nasi eksperci Monika Palmowska i Tomasz Regulski omawiają, jak rozpoznawać i klasyfikować zmiany operacyjne jako restrukturyzację z perspektywy cen transferowych i wycen, jakie obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) mogą się pojawić oraz dlaczego dokumentacja przygotowana „na czas” redukuje ryzyko. Odcinek zawiera praktyczne przykłady (przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów), analizę EBIT, typowe błędy i końcową checklistę działań.
bullets: Definicja restrukturyzacji w kontekście cen transferowych i kryteria jej rozpoznania.
Analiza istotności zmian przez pryzmat wpływu na EBIT i profil działalności.
Znaczenie dokumentacji restrukturyzacji oraz terminowe spełnienie obowiązków raportowych (TPR, MDR).
Rola wycen w procesie restrukturyzacji i omówienie najczęstszych błędów praktycznych.
Przykłady ryzyk i pułapek: przeniesienie produkcji/dystrybucji, transfer pracowników, przeniesienie kontraktów.
takeaways: Przy planowaniu relokacji usług lub infrastruktury uwzględnij ocenę cen transferowych i wpływ na EBIT, aby uniknąć nieoczekiwanych konsekwencji podatkowych.
Zadbaj o kompletną dokumentację restrukturyzacji na etapie planowania — pomoże to w obronie pozycji przed fiskusem i ograniczy stres przy kontroli.
Sprawdź obowiązki raportowe (TPR, MDR) przy zmianach ponadprógowych, by uniknąć sankcji i obowiązków informacyjnych.
Zleć rzetelną wycenę przenoszonych aktywów/umów i ocen transferu pracowników, bo to kluczowy element uzasadnienia ekonomicznego zmian.

bullets: Invites viewers to download Copilot.

Treat the video as a prompt to start hands-on testing rather than a source of implementation guidance.

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (Google's professional services team), says the most expensive mistake in AI is rushing to code without a strategy rather than compute costs. He advises escaping the "coding trap" by reimagining value chains and deploying multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most expensive mistake: rushing to code without a strategy (not computing costs).
Avoid the "coding trap"—prioritize strategy before implementation.
Reimagine value chains to pinpoint where AI delivers real business value.
Deploy multi-agent systems as a practical approach to achieving outcomes.
Insights presented by Marcus Oliver of Google delta (professional services).
takeaways: Prioritize strategy-led AI initiatives when planning infrastructure investments, not just raw compute capacity.
Ensure data center and grid planning account for architectures that support multi-agent, distributed AI workloads.
Engage professional services early to align infrastructure and operations with reimagined value chains.
Avoid overprovisioning compute for premature proofs-of-concept; validate use cases before scaling resources.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description… DeepKeep on Google Cloud is presented as a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions by providing visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure from evolving threats. The video highlights three capabilities—AI Lens for visibility and policy enforcement, AI Red Teaming for stress‑testing against real‑world attacks, and an AI Firewall that secures prompts and responses in real time with built‑in guardrails.
bullets: Unified platform on Google Cloud to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions
AI Lens: visibility and policy application across user–AI interactions
AI Red Teaming: stress‑test applications and models against real‑world attacks
AI Firewall: real‑time protection of prompts and responses with built‑in guardrails
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and defend infrastructure from evolving threats
takeaways: Improved visibility and policy enforcement can reduce risks from unmanaged or shadow AI across the estate
Built‑in red teaming capability supports proactive validation of models and applications before production rollout
Real‑time prompt/response guardrails offer runtime protection to help mitigate operational threats
A unified, cloud‑native solution can simplify governance and operational overhead for securing AI usage

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA GeForce Garage highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community build — u/JeffersonPutnam — who assembled a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig. The description lists the full parts used, including chassis, GPU, CPU, motherboard, RAM, power supply, and AIO cooler.
bullets: Community build winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 system.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
CPU/Motherboard/RAM: Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge, 32GB Teamgroup T-Create DDR5-6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 power supply and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Host and resources: GeForce host Dwight (@_MightyPotato) and full PCPartPicker parts list linked in description.
takeaways: The build uses a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition in a small-form-factor Fractal Era 2 chassis, indicating the GPU was integrated into a compact desktop configuration.
Power provisioning in this build is a Corsair SF1000 SFX unit, showing a high-capacity SFX power solution paired with the RTX 5090 in an SFF build.
Cooling for the system is a Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO, demonstrating a liquid AIO was chosen for thermal management alongside the high-end CPU and GPU.
Platform details (MSI MPG B850I Edge + Ryzen 7 9800X3D + DDR5-6000) are specified, providing an exact component set for compatibility and integration reference.
